varieties.name: Tuscan Blue
varieties.origin: Tuscany, Italy (introduced pre-1948)
varieties.traits: Robust upright growth to 6-7 feet (1.8-2.1m) tall, 2-4 feet wide. Wide, very aromatic dark green leaves. Dark blue-violet flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: High culinary productivity. Favorite of professional chefs for strongest flavor.
varieties.availability: Widely available in Queensland nurseries including Daley's Fruit Trees, Online Plants Australia, Brookfield Gardens humid subtropical climates.

varieties.name: Miss Jessop's Upright
varieties.origin: Heritage variety, England (traditional cultivar, 100+ years)
varieties.traits: Slender, fastigiate (columnar) form to 4-6 feet tall, 2-3 feet wide. Excellent for confined spaces and formal herb gardens.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high. Lower oil levels than broader-leaved types but excellent flavor.
varieties.availability: Available through Australian specialist herb nurseries, Mudbrick Herb Cottage, Jekka's Herbs (UK-sourced).

varieties.name: Blue Spires (Athens Blue Spires)
varieties.origin: Athens, Georgia, USA (introduced 1999 by Allan M. Armitage)
varieties.traits: Strong vertical habit 5-6 feet tall, 3-4 feet wide. Resembles miniature Italian cypress. Clear bright blue flowers, grey-green foliage.
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity. Excellent for fresh and dried culinary use.
varieties.availability: Available through Online Plants Australia, specialty herb suppliers.

varieties.name: Spice Island
varieties.origin: Commercial cultivar, USA (developed for culinary excellence)
varieties.traits: Upright shrub 4-6 feet tall and wide. Very upright form with dramatic presence. Dark blue flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent culinary herb with highest essential oil content. Fresh or dried applications.
varieties.availability: Widely available in Australian nurseries, herb sections of garden centers, Mudbrick Herb Cottage.

varieties.name: Arp
varieties.origin: Arp, Texas, USA (named after town, discovered by Madalene Hill)
varieties.traits: Upright open growth 3-4 feet tall and wide. Most cold-hardy variety (to -10°F/-23°C). Silvery grey-green leaves, pale blue flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity. Lower pinene and camphor levels make it excellent for cooking.
varieties.availability: Available through specialist Australian nurseries, online suppliers.

varieties.name: Salem (Bushy Blue)
varieties.origin: USA commercial cultivar (developed for Pacific Northwest moisture tolerance)
varieties.traits: Compact upright habit 2-3 feet tall, 2 feet wide. Makes excellent hedge. Smoky light green foliage, very blue flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good. More tolerant of moisture than most rosemary varieties.

varieties.name: Hill Hardy (Madalene Hill)
varieties.origin: Texas, USA (discovered as sport mutation of 'Arp' by Madalene Hill)
varieties.traits: Compact, dense upright form 3-5 feet tall and wide. Second most cold-hardy variety (to 0°F/-18°C). Greener foliage than 'Arp'.
varieties.yield_notes: Good culinary productivity. Suitable for fresh or dried applications.
varieties.availability: Available through specialist herb suppliers, occasionally in Australian nurseries.

varieties.name: Gorizia
varieties.origin: Gorizia, Italy (heritage variety from Italian-Slovenian border region)
varieties.traits: Stiffly upright, open form 4-5 feet tall and wide. Very architectural appearance with distinctive character.
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent culinary herb. Sweeter oils make it ideal for kitchen use - one of best culinary varieties.
varieties.availability: Available through Mudbrick Herb Cottage, specialty herb nurseries Australia-wide.

varieties.name: Common Rosemary (Officinalis)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean wild species - original rosemary (thousands of years of cultivation)
varieties.traits: Variable upright growth 3-6 feet tall. Traditional form found in gardens worldwide. Light blue flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable producer. Standard for all culinary, medicinal, and ornamental uses.
varieties.availability: Universally available in all Queensland nurseries, garden centers, Bunnings, independent nurseries.

varieties.name: Majorca Pink (Roseus)
varieties.origin: Balearic Islands, Spain (wild form collected from Majorca, traditional variety)
varieties.traits: Tall loose upright shrub 3-5 feet high, 2-6 feet wide. Semi-weeping habit with cascading branches. Unique lavender-pink to mauve flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate productivity. Good culinary applications despite ornamental focus.
varieties.availability: Available Mudbrick Herb Cottage, Online Plants Australia, specialty nurseries.

varieties.name: Rex
varieties.origin: Commercial cultivar (developed for robust performance)
varieties.traits: Very robust upright variety to 1.2m (4 feet) tall. Strong grower with vigorous habit.
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity due to vigorous growth. Good for regular harvesting.
varieties.availability: Available through Australian specialty nurseries.

varieties.name: Albus (White Rosemary, Albiflorus)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean cultivar (selected for white flowers)
varieties.traits: Upright dense growth 3-4 feet tall and wide. Pure white flowers provide ornamental contrast. Dynamic bloomer with profuse flowering.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity. Suitable for fresh or dried culinary use.
varieties.availability: Available through specialty herb suppliers, Mudbrick Herb Cottage.

varieties.name: Blue Lagoon
varieties.origin: Commercial landscape cultivar
varieties.traits: Low spreading habit 2-4 feet tall, 3-4 feet wide. Bright deep blue flowers in abundance.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Suitable for culinary use and ornamental purposes.

varieties.name: Collingwood Ingram (Benenden Blue, Ingramii)
varieties.origin: England (heritage selection, named after Collingwood Ingram)
varieties.traits: Low spreading 2-2.5 feet tall, 4+ feet wide. Always producing new side growth, woody trunk hidden. Dark blue flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Good for regular harvesting. Popular landscape and culinary variety.
varieties.availability: Available through Australian specialty suppliers.

varieties.name: Lockwood de Forest (Foresteri)
varieties.origin: Santa Barbara, California (from garden of Lockwood and Elizabeth de Forest, heritage selection)
varieties.traits: Prostrate selection from 'Tuscan Blue' lineage. Creates 6-8 feet wide carpet, 1-2 feet tall. Light green foliage, lavender-blue flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity for culinary use despite ground-cover form.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, may require special order in Australia.

varieties.name: Prostrate Rosemary (Prostratus)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean traditional form (centuries-old growth habit)
varieties.traits: Classic trailing rosemary, 2 feet tall, 4-8 feet wide spread. Pale blue flowers. Most commonly used for cascading walls.
varieties.yield_notes: Lower oil content than upright varieties but still usable in cooking.
varieties.availability: Commonly available Queensland nurseries, Brookfield Gardens.

varieties.name: Santa Barbara (Trailing Rosemary, Creeping)
varieties.origin: Santa Barbara, California (heritage landscape selection)
varieties.traits: Low mounding 0.3m (12 inches) tall, spreads 3x its height. Pale blue flowers. Classic trailing effect stunning on walls.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Culinary use possible but primarily ornamental.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, Nature's Herb Farm (US), may need sourcing in Australia.

varieties.name: Huntington Carpet (Huntington Blue Carpet)
varieties.origin: Huntington Botanical Gardens, California (cultivar developed for ground cover)
varieties.traits: True prostrate form, 10-12 inches tall (30cm), 3-6 feet wide spread. Exceptionally bright blue flowers, dense low growth. Dark green conifer-like foliage.
varieties.yield_notes: Good for culinary use despite prostrate form. Intensely fragrant.
varieties.availability: Available Tablelands Nurseries Online, specialty suppliers. True form difficult to find in Australia.

varieties.name: Irene (Renzels)
varieties.origin: Commercial trailing cultivar
varieties.traits: Prostrate habit 12-18 inches tall, 24-36 inches wide. Long trailing stems up to 3 feet. Intense dark blue flowers - blooms longer than most varieties.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate productivity. Suitable for culinary use.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, Nature's Herb Farm network.

varieties.name: Ken Taylor (Taylor's Blue)
varieties.origin: Commercial landscape cultivar
varieties.traits: Similar to Collingwood Ingram but slightly shorter, more trailing. Showy dark blue flowers. Shrubby trailing habit.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Suitable for landscape and occasional culinary use.
varieties.availability: Specialty landscape nurseries, may require sourcing.

varieties.name: McConnell's Blue
varieties.origin: Commercial prostrate cultivar
varieties.traits: Low-growing creeping form. Excellent ground cover. Light green foliage with pale blue flowers in abundance.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate culinary potential despite ground-cover form.
varieties.availability: Specialty herb suppliers Australia.

varieties.name: Pine Scented Rosemary
varieties.origin: Selected cultivar for Indian growing conditions
varieties.traits: Upright growth 2-4 feet. Specifically recommended for Indian climate by cultivation guides. Soft leaf texture.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in warm climates. Best culinary variety for India.

varieties.name: Blue Boy (Dwarf)
varieties.origin: Commercial dwarf cultivar
varieties.traits: Compact dwarf 12 inches (30cm) tall. Small leaves, tight growth. Good for containers. Recommended for cooking in India.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate due to compact size. Continuous harvesting possible.
varieties.availability: Indian online plant retailers, agricultural stores, tropical monsoon climates nurseries.

varieties.name: Spice Islands (for India)
varieties.origin: USA cultivar, adapted for Indian conditions
varieties.traits: Upright 3-5 feet. Commonly used in cooking in India according to cultivation guides. Most aromatic variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent for culinary use. Highly aromatic for Indian cuisine applications.
varieties.availability: Urban Platter India, The Affordable Organic Store, specialty herb suppliers India.

varieties.name: White Rosemary (Albus for India)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean cultivar recommended for Indian conditions
varieties.traits: Upright 2-4 feet. White flowers. Listed as commonly used for cooking in India.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in suitable conditions.
varieties.availability: Indian nurseries, online plant stores, herb sections.

varieties.name: Common Rosemary (Officinalis)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean species - grown in India with adaptations
varieties.traits: Standard upright 2-5 feet. Most widely available in India. Blue flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable with proper care. All-purpose culinary herb.
varieties.availability: Widely available Indian nurseries, Ugaoo, Seeds India, local plant shops tropical monsoon climates.

varieties.name: Arp (Landscape)
varieties.origin: Texas, USA - used in Indian landscapes
varieties.traits: Upright 3-4 feet. Grey-green leaves, pale blue flowers. Listed as landscape variety for Indian gardens.
varieties.yield_notes: Good culinary potential despite ornamental use.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries India, may require online ordering.

varieties.name: Dancing Waters
varieties.origin: Commercial landscape cultivar
varieties.traits: Trailing/spreading habit. Listed as landscape plant for Indian gardens.
varieties.yield_notes: Primarily ornamental. Limited culinary use.
varieties.availability: Specialty landscape nurseries India, limited availability.

varieties.name: Golden Rain (Joyce DeBaggio)
varieties.origin: USA cultivar (named after Joyce DeBaggio)
varieties.traits: Compact 2-3 feet. Yellow-gold variegated leaves. Dark blue flowers. Landscape variety for Indian gardens.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Primarily grown for ornamental value.
varieties.availability: Specialty herb suppliers India, may need special order.

varieties.name: Pink Rosemary (Majorca/Roseus)
varieties.origin: Balearic Islands, Spain - grown as landscape plant in India
varieties.traits: Semi-weeping 3-5 feet. Pink to lavender flowers. Listed as landscape variety for Indian gardens.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate culinary potential but primarily ornamental.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, online herb retailers India.

varieties.name: White (Landscape variety)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean cultivar used in Indian landscaping
varieties.traits: Upright 2-4 feet. Pure white flowers provide ornamental contrast. Listed as landscape variety for Indian gardens.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Dual-purpose ornamental and culinary.
